articles.title: Novelis Greensboro Recycling Facility Resumes Casting After March Explosion
articles.summary: Novelis confirmed its Greensboro, Georgia recycling facility has resumed casting operations following the March 2026 explosion, with full production expected within weeks. The restart removes a near-term supply constraint on North American flat-rolled output.

articles.title: Constellium Expands Neuf-Brisach Automotive Sheet Capacity by 15%
articles.summary: Constellium announced a EUR 120M investment to expand automotive body sheet capacity at Neuf-Brisach by 15%, targeting German OEM demand for EV body-in-white applications from 2027. The expansion directly competes with Novelis Sierre and Nachterstedt in the European automotive sheet segment.

articles.title: European Aluminium Beverage Can Recycling Reaches Record 76.3%
articles.summary: European aluminium beverage can recycling hit a record 76.3% in 2023, with Germany at 99% driven by its deposit return scheme. The sustained high recycling rates underpin feedstock supply for closed-loop can sheet production, directly supporting Novelis's 3x30 recycled content targets.

articles.title: Euranimi Petitions for CBAM Declaration Deadline Extension to December 2027
articles.summary: Euranimi petitioned the European Commission to extend the CBAM emissions declaration deadline for 2026 imports from September to December 2027, citing that verification of non-EU installations cannot begin until January 2027. Without the extension, default emission values could inflate aluminium import costs to EUR 600-800/t versus EUR 0-100/t with verified data.

articles.title: LME Aluminium Rises as Gulf Supply Disruptions Persist
articles.summary: LME three-month aluminium rose to $3,241/t as Strait of Hormuz disruptions continue to constrain Gulf smelter output, with Qatalum at 60% capacity and Alba running 19% below nameplate. The European duty-paid premium widened to $375-395/t as buyers compete for non-Middle Eastern origin metal.
